SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain

Class RobustOptimizationStudy

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain.RobustOptimizationStudy
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class RobustOptimizationStudy extends Object implements Serializable
Evaluates the robustness of operating decisions against parameter uncertainty.

A setpoint that is optimal for the nominal case can be fragile: small shifts in feed composition, reservoir pressure or ambient temperature may erode its value or push the plant infeasible. This study couples a Monte-Carlo scenario set with an objective evaluator to report the value distribution (P10/P50/P90, mean) and the fraction of scenarios in which a decision stays feasible. It can also pick, from a set of candidate decisions, the one that maximises the median value subject to a feasibility-confidence requirement — i.e. a chance-constrained selection such as "maximise P50 value while remaining feasible in at least 90% of scenarios".

Scenarios may be supplied explicitly (deterministic, reproducible) or drawn from a RobustOptimizationStudy.ScenarioSampler. Outcomes are produced by a RobustOptimizationStudy.ScenarioEvaluator that, for a given decision and scenario, returns the objective and whether the plant is feasible (typically by configuring and running a NeqSim flowsheet and pricing it with ValueChainObjective).

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static class 
    RobustOptimizationStudy.RobustResult
    Robustness summary of one decision across all scenarios.
    static interface 
    RobustOptimizationStudy.ScenarioEvaluator
    Functional evaluator returning the outcome of a decision under a scenario.
    static class 
    RobustOptimizationStudy.ScenarioOutcome
    Outcome of one decision under one scenario.
    static interface 
    RobustOptimizationStudy.ScenarioSampler
    Functional sampler that draws an uncertain-parameter scenario.

      Explicit scenarios supplied by the caller.

  • Method Details

    • addScenario

      public RobustOptimizationStudy addScenario(double[] scenario)
      Adds an explicit scenario vector.
      Parameters:
      scenario - the scenario vector (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this study for method chaining

    • setSampler

      public RobustOptimizationStudy setSampler(RobustOptimizationStudy.ScenarioSampler sampler, int count)
      Sets the sampler used to draw scenarios when no explicit scenarios are supplied.
      Parameters:
      sampler - the scenario sampler
      count - the number of scenarios to draw (must be positive)
      Returns:
      this study for method chaining

    • setSeed

      public RobustOptimizationStudy setSeed(long seed)
      Sets the random seed used for sampling.
      Parameters:
      seed - the random seed
      Returns:
      this study for method chaining

    • setRequiredConfidence

      public RobustOptimizationStudy setRequiredConfidence(double requiredConfidence)
      Sets the required feasibility fraction for chance-constrained selection.
      Parameters:
      requiredConfidence - the required feasible fraction in the range [0, 1]
      Returns:
      this study for method chaining

    • buildScenarios

      private List<double[]> buildScenarios()
      Builds the scenario set, drawing from the sampler if no explicit scenarios were supplied.
      Returns:
      the list of scenario vectors to evaluate

    • evaluateDecision

      public RobustOptimizationStudy.RobustResult evaluateDecision(double[] decision, RobustOptimizationStudy.ScenarioEvaluator evaluator)
      Evaluates the robustness of a single decision across all scenarios.
      Parameters:
      decision - the operating decision vector (must not be null)
      evaluator - the scenario evaluator (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the robustness summary of the decision

    • selectRobust

      public RobustOptimizationStudy.RobustResult selectRobust(List<double[]> candidates, RobustOptimizationStudy.ScenarioEvaluator evaluator)
      Selects, from a set of candidate decisions, the one maximising the median objective while meeting the feasibility-confidence requirement.

      If no candidate meets the confidence requirement, the candidate with the highest feasible fraction is returned as a fallback.

      Parameters:
      candidates - the candidate decision vectors (must not be null or empty)
      evaluator - the scenario evaluator (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the robustness summary of the selected decision

    • percentile

      private static double percentile(double[] sorted, double p)
      Computes a percentile of a sorted array using linear interpolation.
      Parameters:
      sorted - the sorted values (ascending, non-empty)
      p - the percentile in the range [0, 100]
      Returns:
      the interpolated percentile value
